title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text: Historical backtest (no orders placed). Daily OHLCV bars pulled from IBKR TWS for 50 equities + 7 macro indices + 7 sector ETFs, all spanning 5 years (2021-01-01 to present). The strategy is refit at every fold boundary so no future data leaks into any fold’s training.

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Strategy Logic
The strategy is the Keltner Squeeze Breakout: Bollinger Bands (20, 2 std) collapse inside Keltner Channels (20, 1.5 ATR) for at least one bar (the “squeeze on” regime of compressed volatility), then fire when the Bollinger Bands expand back outside the Keltner envelope. On the fire bar the close must also pierce the upper Keltner band (long) or the lower band (short). Two participation filters gate the trade: the fire-bar volume must be at least 1.3x its 20-day average, and ADX(14) on that bar must be >= 20 so the squeeze resolves into a real trend rather than chop. Exits: +4 × ATR profit target, -2 × ATR stop-loss, or 20 trading days timeout (market-on-close). Every trade pays IBKR-tiered commission, SEC and FINRA fees on sells, 5 bps per side of slippage, and a 3% annual short-borrow for shorts. The period’s aggregate net profit is taxed at the 30% short-term capital-gains rate.

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Asset Selection
I screened a twenty-ticker thematic universe across the seven structural stories that drove single-name volatility in 2024 and 2025: AI and semis (NVDA, AVGO, SMCI, AMD), nuclear renaissance (CCJ, VST, OKLO, SMR), Bitcoin proxies (MSTR, COIN, MARA), quantum computing (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS), defense and autonomy (PLTR, LMT, RTX), GLP-1 weight-loss (LLY, NVO), and space (RKLB). Each candidate gets a Breakout Quality Score:
\[
\mathrm{BQS} = \left( \frac{n}{N} \right) \cdot (1 - f_{\text{false}}) \cdot \operatorname{med}(\Delta P_{10})
\]
where n is the count of confirmed squeeze breakouts in 2024, N is the number of training bars, f_false is the fraction of breakouts that close back inside the channel within three days, and med(dP_10) is the median 10-bar direction-adjusted return after the signal. BQS rewards assets where breakouts are frequent, clean, and follow through. The strategy backtests squeeze fires across the entire 20-name universe, with BQS surfacing which names are the highest-quality regime for that setup.

BQS ranking (2024 training)

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Breakout Definition
A confirmed squeeze-breakout signal fires on bar t when all five conditions hold:

Squeeze on at t-1: Bollinger Bands lie fully inside Keltner Channels.
Squeeze off at t: Bollinger Bands expand beyond Keltner (volatility released).
Directional break at close(t): close pierces the upper KC (long) or lower KC (short).
Volume confirmation: bar-t volume >= VOLUME_MULT = 1.3 × 20-day rolling average volume.
Trend confirmation: ADX(14) >= ADX_MIN = 20 on bar t.

Reference bands use a t-1 shift (current bar never part of its own reference). Execution happens at the open of bar t+1 (no look-ahead).

Plain-English description of detect_breakouts
The function detect_breakouts(df) in breakout.py takes a daily OHLCV frame and returns a single series aligned to the input dates. On each bar it answers “did a confirmed Keltner-Squeeze breakout happen today?” with +1 (long), -1 (short), or 0 (no signal). Internally it builds the Bollinger Bands (SMA-20 ± 2 std) and Keltner Channels (EMA-20 ± 1.5 ATR), flags “squeeze on” bars where BB lies fully inside KC, and fires a signal when the squeeze transitions from on (t-1) to off (t) and the close of t pierces the appropriate Keltner band. It then adds the two participation confirmations (volume >= 1.3 × 20-day avg, ADX(14) >= 20). The t-1 index offset is the data-leakage guardrail: today’s close is compared against the Keltner and Bollinger levels computed on the previous 20 bars, not the last 20 bars inclusive of today, so today’s breakout is never part of its own reference set.

title: HW5: Breakout Strategy
text:
Exit Attribution (OOS)
Every trade ends as one of three outcomes: profit target (+4 x ATR hit), stop-loss (-2 x ATR hit), or timeout (neither hit within 20 trading days, closed at market). The donut shows the mix. A strategy dominated by timeouts and profit targets is a “momentum rider”; a strategy dominated by stop-losses is a “fakeout machine” and needs rethinking.


        
        
        
Exit-type breakdown, OOS 2025

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Slippage Sensitivity
Stitched OOS rerun at 5 / 10 / 15 bps per side. A strategy that degrades gracefully is execution-robust; one that collapses at 15 bps is spread-dependent.

title: HW5: Breakout Strategy
text:
Walk-Forward Discipline
Training ran on 2024 daily bars only. A grid search over LOOKBACK in {10,15,20,25,30,40}, STOP_ATR in {1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0}, and PROFIT_ATR in {3.0, 4.0, 5.0, 6.0} selected the combination with the best 2024 Sharpe subject to a minimum of 3 trades (so we do not overfit on a single lucky trade). Those parameters were then frozen and applied to 2025 OOS without any re-fitting. To pressure-test: parameter sensitivity at a one-step grid around each tuned param, a 1000-iteration block-bootstrap CI on the OOS Sharpe, H1 vs H2 OOS sub-period stability, and the slippage stress table above.

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Assumptions & Replicability

Data. IBKR TWS via shinybroker, 5 years of daily bars cached as parquet under FinTech533/Homeworks/HW5/data/. Each symbol covers 2021-01-04 through present. Ordinary trading hours only. Macro indices (VIX, VIX3M, TNX, FVX, IRX, TYX, SPY) and seven sector ETFs (XLK, XLV, XLU, XLF, ITA, URA, IBIT) are cached for exogenous feature construction.
Execution. Signals at close of bar t. Execution at open of bar t+1. If stop and target touch on the same bar, stop triggers first (conservative).
Slippage. 5 bps per side (10 bps round-trip), stressed to 10 and 15 bps.
Commission. IBKR Pro tiered at $0.0035/share, $0.35 min per order, 1% of notional cap.
Regulatory fees (sells). SEC Section 31 at 0.00278% of notional + FINRA TAF at $0.000166/share capped at $8.30.
Short-borrow. 3% annualized linear accrual on notional × hold_days / 252.
Taxes. Short-term capital gains at 30%, applied to period aggregate net positive PnL. Losses offset gains within the period. No carry-forwards modeled.
Position sizing. Fixed $10,000 notional per trade, whole shares only (rounded down), one open position per ticker at a time. Strategies run in parallel across 16 tickers.
ML. StandardScaler fit on each fold’s training features only. Logistic Regression with class_weight='balanced', solver='liblinear', C=1. Threshold at MIN_PROBABILITY = 0.50 (frozen, never retuned across folds). Features strictly exogenous (Vestal’s Law).

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Discussion
The engineering change that matters most here is the expanding walk-forward refit. A single frozen split is pedagogically unsatisfying: the LR never adapts to new regimes, and a ~10-trade OOS sample gives Hoeffding no room to tighten. Four folds produce a multi-year OOS stream, let the Hoeffding monitor’s 1/sqrt(N) convergence actually move, and make the LR coefficient table a real regime-drift diagnostic rather than a 14-feature snapshot. The BQS drift table plays the same role for ticker selection: same top-5 across folds means the breakout regime is persistent; leaderboard churn means BQS is reactive to recent vol and needs to be disclosed as such.
Claims kept bounded. This is a small-sample backtest of a low-frequency setup on 50 names. With ~N OOS trades pooled across the stream, the Hoeffding bounds are meaningful but still loose , they would tighten further with longer history or a wider universe. The strategy’s edge (if any) is in participation and regime, not in cross-sectional alpha: Vestal’s exogenous features decide whether the macro tape is inviting a squeeze to resolve into trend, and the Hoeffding monitor decides whether yesterday’s regime still holds.
Natural extensions for the final project. Top-3 BQS portfolio (trade only the top-ranked names in each fold’s updated BQS table) with correlation-aware sizing. Kelly position sizing conditional on LR probability. Monte-Carlo block-bootstrap of the stitched OOS stream to place confidence intervals on pooled Sharpe. Regime-adaptive ATR multipliers (widen stops when VIX is elevated, tighten in quiet regimes).

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Exogenous ML Filter (Vestal’s Verbal Improvement)
Vestal’s critique: a model trained on a ticker’s own history is just learning its own noise. The filter therefore asks “is the macro environment conducive to momentum continuation today?” using strictly exogenous features (no ticker OHLCV enters the feature vector).

Yield curve spline coefficients (4) , degree-3 polynomial fit to {0.25y, 5y, 10y, 30y} Treasury yields (IBKR’s IRX/FVX/TNX/TYX indices). Level, slope, curvature.
VIX term structure (4) , VIX level, VIX3M level, VIX3M - VIX spread (contango or backwardation), 20-day VIX change.
IV - RV spread (3) , VIX (market IV proxy) minus 30-day realized volatility of SPY.
Broad-market regime (1) , 20-day SPY return.
Sector relative strength (2) , 5-day ticker return minus 5-day sector ETF return (relative measure only), sector 5-day return.

Per-fold refit. Every feature lookup uses the entry_date - 1 value. StandardScaler is fit on training-fold features only, then transformed on the test fold. Logistic Regression trained with class_weight='balanced', liblinear solver, C=1. Trained LR’s predict_proba attached to every trade for transparency.

LR coefficient stability table
Consistent sign and magnitude across folds is a signal that the feature is doing real work. Sign flips across folds indicate noise.

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Hoeffding Regime Monitor (Egger & Vestal 2025)
Two one-sided monitors run on the concatenated OOS trade stream across all 4 folds (~N trades pooled). Under H0 (the pooled training-year regime persists), Hoeffding’s Inequality bounds the probability of observing an underperformance as large as the realized mu - X̄:
\[
P(\bar X - \mu \ge t \mid H_0) \le \exp\!\left(\frac{-2 t^2 N_{\text{eff}}}{(b - a)^2}\right)
\]
N_eff = min(N × (1 - ρ)/(1 + ρ), N) corrects for trade-outcome autocorrelation. Alert thresholds: 50% (amber) / 25% (orange) / 10% (red → halt).

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Expanding Walk-Forward Discipline
The assignment literally says “rolling walk-forward window… helps demonstrate robustness and reduces overfitting.” Instead of a single frozen train/test split, the engine is refit at four fold boundaries:



Fold
Training window
OOS test year




1
2021
2022


2
2021-2022
2023


3
2021-2023
2024


4
2021-2024
2025



Training sets expand (never throw away history), so Fold 4’s LR is trained on every trade that was knowable by end-of-2024. At each fold: (1) BQS is recomputed from the training years, (2) StandardScaler is re-fit on training features only, (3) Logistic Regression is re-trained on training labels only. The strategy’s design parameters (BB/KC lookback, ATR multiples, exits, position sizing, cost model) and MIN_PROBABILITY = 0.50 are frozen across all folds , those are not learned from data, and retuning them per fold would re-introduce the overfitting the walk-forward is supposed to prevent.

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Asset Selection: Survivorship-Clean Universe
Selection rule, committed ex-ante before any OOS result was viewed: “Top 50 US equities by Jan 1, 2021 dollar volume that traded continuously from Jan 1, 2020 through Apr 17, 2026, excluding ETFs and ADRs.” The rule is mechanical: a pre-dated liquidity ranking on a cutoff (Jan 1, 2021) that precedes the test windows, combined with a survivorship filter. The final list is 50 US-primary listings (14 thematic survivors from the original design plus 36 large-cap mega-caps). ADRs (e.g. NVO, CCJ cross-listing) are dropped on principle. Each name is scored by Breakout Quality Score per fold:
\[
\mathrm{BQS} = \left( \frac{n}{N} \right) \cdot (1 - f_{\text{false}}) \cdot \operatorname{med}(\Delta P_{10})
\]
where n is the confirmed squeeze breakouts in the training window, N is the number of training bars, f_false is the fraction of breakouts that close back inside the channel within three days, and med(dP_10) is the median 10-bar direction-adjusted return after the signal.

BQS drift across folds


        
        
        


                            
                                            


Same names across columns = stable regime. Leaderboard shuffling = BQS is reactive to recent regime (disclose that in reading).

text:
Three-line Equity Curve: Gross vs Net vs After-Tax (2022-2025 stitched)
The continuous equity line below is built by summing per-ticker mark-to-market deltas across all 4 folds. Dotted vertical lines mark fold boundaries; the Gross line adds back cumulative friction, the Net line is after all costs, and the After-Tax line deducts 30% from any positive net PnL.

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Cost Exposure
Every trade pays friction (commission + regulatory fees + slippage + short-borrow) and the aggregate positive net PnL is taxed. Gross → friction → tax → net.

title: HW5: Keltner Squeeze Breakout, Expanding Walk-Forward, Exogenous ML, Hoeffding Regime Monitor
text:
Engineering Philosophy
The assignment encourages keeping core logic simple. The base strategy here, Keltner Squeeze with vol+ADX confirmation, is intentionally that simple. The additional layers (exogenous LR, Hoeffding monitor, walk-forward refit, friction engine) are presented as labeled extensions on top of the base, not as the strategy itself. Each can be removed without affecting the breakout detector or trade simulation.
